title: Tanker report - Week 21
summary:
VLCCs
In the Middle East Gulf, rates for 270,000 tonnes going long east have been hovering in the low WS 50s, although there has been a deal recently concluded by Chinese charterers at WS 55 but this is a run to Kiire with somewhat extended laytime having been agreed. Earlier in the week, a voyage to Thailand went at WS 52 again basis 270,000 tonnes quantity, while a 2000 built ship fixed another run to Thailand at WS 49.5. Going west, Exxon are reported to have fixed 280,000 tonnes to US Gulf at WS 21 Suez/Suez with option cape/cape at WS 23 although this was on a ship ex drydock.

children.title: VLCCs
children.summary: In the Middle East Gulf, rates for 270,000 tonnes going long east have been hovering in the low WS 50s, although there has been a deal recently concluded by Chinese charterers at WS 55 but this is a run to Kiire with somewhat extended laytime having been agreed. Earlier in the week, a voyage to Thailand went at WS 52 again basis 270,000 tonnes quantity, while a 2000 built ship fixed another run to Thailand at WS 49.5.

children.title: Suezmaxes
children.summary: In contrast to the VLCCs, suezmax rates fared better this week. In West Africa, rates for 130,000 tonnes have continued to firm throughout the week, with the market now at around WS 95, representing a gain of around 20 points from the start of the week.

children.title: Aframaxes
children.summary: In the Mediterranean, it has been a volatile week. The start of the week saw rates continuing to firm with to mid WS 140s in the Med and WS 150 done from Black Sea, albeit on a replacement cargo.

children.title: Panamaxes
children.summary: It has been an uneventful week in the 55,000 tonnes, ARA to US Gulf trade, with rates largely unchanged at WS 115 with Skikda rates at similar levels.

children.title: Clean
children.summary: It has been a lacklustre week in the Middle East Gulf with the market for 75,000 tonnes to Japan losing around WS 5 points to settle at WS 85. It has been a similar story in the 55,000 tonnes AG/Japan trade where rates have stagnated at around WS 97.5 region.
